meta.title: BST Function Recipes — Compound Operations on the Periodic Table
meta.description: Chemical-style formulas for combining function families via n_C=5 bonding operations. Each recipe takes two sectors and produces a result sector via set union of integers.
meta.bonding_operations_note: 5 = n_C closure operations on the function catalog
meta.closure_property: All operations map catalog entries to catalog entries (closure under set union)

operations.name: Multiplication
operations.meijer_g_rule: Union of integer sets; G-type follows from combined parameter count

operations.name: Convolution
operations.meijer_g_rule: Union of integer sets; Meijer G convolution theorem applies
operations.ac_depth_rule: max(d1, d2) + 1 if crossing period boundary, else max(d1, d2)

operations.name: Integration
operations.meijer_g_rule: Union of integer sets; integration of G-functions is a G-function
operations.physical_meaning: Partition function, path integral, expectation value

operations.name: Differentiation
operations.meijer_g_rule: Union of integer sets; derivatives of G-functions are G-functions

operations.name: Mellin Transform
operations.description: Mellin transform converting between multiplicative and additive structure
operations.meijer_g_rule: Mellin of G is G with shifted parameters

recipes.name: Bergman kernel
recipes.notes: Self-interaction of topology. The kernel that reproduces all functions in the catalog.

recipes.name: Bessel from topology+compact
recipes.notes: Topology meets compact space → wave mechanics. First composite: 10 = 2×5.

recipes.name: Casimir from topology+color
recipes.notes: COLLAPSE: 2×3=6=C_2. The CR sector collapses into K because C_2 absorbs rank×color. This is NOT period 2 — it stays elementary.

recipes.name: Rainbow angle
recipes.notes: Field theory meets cosmology. The 42-degree rainbow angle is a BST rational.

recipes.name: Nuclear binding
recipes.notes: Compact space × Casimir → nuclear physics. First composite: 30 = 5×6.

recipes.name: Hypergeometric from three sectors
recipes.notes: The DKR sector has the most composites of any triple: rank×compact×Casimir covers nuclear binding, atomic physics, wave mechanics.

recipes.name: Proton mass
recipes.formula: R × C × D × K × G → CDGKR
recipes.physical_result: m_p = 6π^5 m_e — requires all five integers
recipes.notes: The proton mass formula engages every BST integer. Universal sector.

recipes.name: Gravitational coupling
recipes.physical_result: Newton's G — four integers, missing color
recipes.notes: Gravity uses rank, n_C, C_2, g but NOT N_c (no color charge in gravity). The DGKR sector is 'SM without color'.

recipes.name: Fine structure constant
recipes.notes: The Painlevé wrench chain reduces all five integers to one constant. The price of participation.

recipes.name: Fermi scale
recipes.notes: The electroweak scale emerges from color × genus × rank.

recipes.name: Strong coupling
recipes.notes: The strong force coupling is genus over four times compact dimension.

recipes.name: Weak mixing angle
recipes.notes: The Weinberg angle from color and compact dimensions.

recipes.name: Dark matter fraction
recipes.notes: Dark matter is compact dimensions over Farey sequence length.

recipes.name: Metabolic 3/4 scaling
recipes.notes: Biology emerges from color/rank. Brain uses 19.1% = f_c of metabolic budget.

collapse_rules.explanation: rank × N_c = 2 × 3 = 6 = C_2. Products of rank and color collapse into the Casimir sector.
collapse_rules.consequence: The CR sector has zero composites in the prime residue table — all are absorbed by K.

collapse_rules.explanation: rank × N_c × n_C: since 2×3=6=C_2, the triple CDR rearranges as DK (compact × Casimir).
collapse_rules.consequence: Some period-3 sectors effectively reduce to period-2 behavior.

collapse_rules.explanation: All products of 2×3×5×6×7 contain 6=2×3=C_2, so they factorize into products involving C_2 and fall into known lower sectors.
collapse_rules.consequence: The universal sector is theoretically complete but has zero unique composites in the prime residue table.

noble_gases.description: The 6 Painlevé transcendents (PI-PVI) are the 'noble gases' — irreducible functions that won't bond (reduce) to simpler forms.
noble_gases.analogy: Noble gases have filled shells → stable. Painlevé transcendents have maximal differential Galois groups → irreducible. Both are complete because they are full.
noble_gases.boundary_role: They define the boundary of the periodic table — the wall between computable and non-computable.
